text:
{
  "score": 1,
   "feedback": "The student answer demonstrates a basic understanding of the difference between pre-colonial barter trade and modern trade, but lacks detail and clarity. The answer correctly states that people exchanged goods in barter trade and use money in modern trade, but does not fully explain the significance of this difference or provide examples. To improve, the student could provide more context and elaborate on the characteristics of each type of trade.",
   "correctedAnswer": "In pre-colonial Rwanda, people used barter trade to exchange goods without using money. Modern trade is different because it involves the use of money and other forms of exchange, such as credit and debit cards.",
   "keyConceptsPresent": ["barter trade", "modern trade", "use of money"],
   "keyConceptsMissing": ["characteristics of barter trade", "forms of exchange in modern trade", "significance of the difference between pre-colonial and modern trade"],
   "confidenceLevel": "medium"
}

The answer correctly states that people exchanged goods in barter trade and use money in modern trade, but does not fully explain the significance of this difference or provide examples. To improve, the student could provide more context and elaborate on the characteristics of each type of trade.
